title: Osteospermum asperulum

intro: Osteospermum asperulum, also known as Cape daisy, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow or purple flowers and a sprawling habit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Osteospermum asperulum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Osteospermum asperulum is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wound healing.
